1→# Legacy Template Modernization Documentation
     2→
     3→**Branch:** `jb/17083-modernize-legacy-templates-pt1`  
     4→**Date Started:** December 31, 2025
     5→
     6→## Purpose
     7→
     8→This documentation captures the specific modernization changes made to legacy WordPress page templates in the HoneLandingPages theme. It serves as a reference for AI assistants (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor) to replicate the exact same pattern across other legacy templates.
     9→
    10→## Overview
    11→
    12→The modernization process converts standalone HTML templates (with embedded head/body/footer) into modular WordPress templates that leverage:
    13→- Centralized `header.php` and `footer.php`
    14→- Template-specific SCSS that imports legacy styles
    15→- Proper WordPress enqueuing system
    16→- Consistent template variable structure
    17→
    18→## Templates Modernized
    19→
    20→1. ✅ `page-article-trt-pmax.php` (commits: 3f7e514, bd428b7)
    21→2. ✅ `page-mens-hrt-cc-buybox.php` (commits: c0a2e5e, dec2d4c, 424010b)
    22→3. ✅ `page-mens-hrt-cc-why-seek.php` (commit: cdec2e5)
    23→4. ✅ `page-overview-multiquiz.php`
    24→5. ✅ `page-overview-light-rebrand.php` (January 5, 2026)
    25→
    26→## Documentation Structure
    27→
    28→```
    29→.local/template-modernization/
    30→├── README.md                           # This file - overview
    31→├── INDEX.md                            # Complete documentation index
    32→├── MODERNIZATION_PATTERN.md            # Step-by-step process
    33→├── BEFORE_AFTER_EXAMPLES.md            # Real code transformations
    34→├── QUICK_REFERENCE.md                  # Fast reference guide
    35→├── COMMON_ISSUES.md                    # Troubleshooting guide
    36→└── changes/                            # Individual template changelogs
    37→    ├── page-article-trt-pmax.md
    38→    ├── page-mens-hrt-cc-buybox.md
    39→    ├── page-mens-hrt-cc-why-seek.md
    40→    ├── page-overview-multiquiz.md
    41→    └── page-overview-light-rebrand.md
    42→```
    43→
    44→## Key Changes Summary
    45→
    46→### 1. PHP Template Structure
    47→- **Before:** Full HTML document with inline `<head>`, `<body>`, tracking scripts
    48→- **After:** Template header with variables, `get_header()`, content only, `get_footer()`
    49→
    50→### 2. Asset Management
    51→- **Before:** Inline CSS/JS links, manual enqueuing
    52→- **After:** `wp_enqueue_script()` with template-specific bundles, CSS handled by functions.php
    53→
    54→### 3. CSS Architecture
    55→- **Before:** Monolithic CSS file copied inline or referenced directly
    56→- **After:** Page-specific SCSS that imports legacy styles as a mixin, allows overrides
    57→
    58→### 4. Global Variables
    59→- **Before:** None, or page-specific
    60→- **After:** `$class_names[]`, `$linkbar_vars[]`, `$template_name`
    61→
    62→### 5. Template Parts
    63→- **Before:** Inline header/nav HTML
    64→- **After:** `linkbar.php` and `navbar.php` template parts called from `header.php`
    65→
    66→## Quick Start for New Template
    67→
    68→1. Read `MODERNIZATION_PATTERN.md` for step-by-step process
    69→2. Check `BEFORE_AFTER_EXAMPLES.md` for code patterns
    70→3. Look at similar completed template in `changes/`
    71→4. Apply the pattern
    72→5. Document your changes in `changes/[template-name].md`
    73→
    74→## Files Modified
    75→
    76→### Per Template
    77→- `src/page-[template].php` - Main template file
    78→- `src/page-[template].old.php` - Backup of original
    79→- `src/static/scss/pages/[template].scss` - New SCSS structure
    80→- `src/static/scss/legacy/styles/[legacy-file].scss` - Extracted legacy styles (if new)
    81→
    82→### Global
    83→- `src/functions.php` - Added template name to HONE_PAGE_TEMPLATES array
    84→
    85→## Build System
    86→
    87→The project uses `esbuild.build.mjs` to compile:
    88→- SCSS → CSS (with sourcemaps)
    89→- JavaScript bundles (with minification)
    90→
    91→Build outputs go to `/build/` directory.
    92→
    93→## Testing Checklist
    94→
    95→After modernizing a template:
    96→- [ ] Visual regression - page looks identical
    97→- [ ] All interactive elements work
    98→- [ ] JavaScript functionality intact
    99→- [ ] Forms submit correctly
   100→- [ ] Sticky CTA behaves correctly
   101→- [ ] Quiz/interactive elements work (if applicable)
   102→- [ ] Mobile responsive
   103→- [ ] Console has no errors
   104→

1→# Legacy Template Modernization Pattern 2→ 3→This document describes the exact step-by-step process used to modernize legacy templates, based on actual changes made to `page-article-trt-pmax.php`, `page-mens-hrt-cc-buybox.php`, and `page-mens-hrt-cc-why-seek.php`. 4→ 5→## Step-by-Step Process 6→ 7→### Step 1: Create Backup 8→ 9→```bash 10→cp src/page-[template].php src/page-[template].old.php 11→``` 12→ 13→**Update the template name in the `.old.php` file** to differentiate it in WordPress: 14→ 15→```php 16→// Change this: 17→ 18→ 19→// To this: 20→ 21→``` 22→ 23→This ensures the backup template appears separately in the WordPress template dropdown and won't conflict with the modernized version. 24→ 25→### Step 2: Extract Legacy Styles 26→ 27→If this template uses a unique CSS file that hasn't been extracted yet: 28→ 29→1. Create `src/static/scss/legacy/styles/[original-css-name].scss` 30→2. Copy the original CSS content 31→3. **Remove all @font-face declarations** (fonts are already in main.css) 32→4. 1→# Common Issues & Solutions 2→ 3→This document tracks common problems encountered during template modernization and their solutions. 4→ 5→## Issue #1: PHP String Escaping 6→ 7→**Discovered:** page-overview-multiquiz.php modernization 8→ 9→### Problem 10→ 11→Original templates may contain "smart quotes" or curly apostrophes (Unicode characters `'` and `"`) that don't conflict with PHP string delimiters. When these are converted to straight ASCII quotes (`'` and `"`), they MUST be escaped or they'll cause syntax errors. 12→ 13→### Symptoms 14→ 15→- PHP parse errors: "unexpected token" or "unterminated string" 16→- Strings appearing to end prematurely 17→- Page fails to load with 500 error 18→ 19→### Example 20→ 21→```php 22→// Original (curly apostrophe) - works 23→'body' => 'I'm able to share my passion...' // ✅ No conflict 24→ 25→// After text normalization - BREAKS 26→'body' => 'I'm able to share my passion...' // ❌ String ends at I' 27→ 28→// Correct fix - escape it 29→'body' => 'I\'m able to share my passion...' // ✅ Works 30→``` 31→ 32→### Solution 33→ 34→When modernizing templates: 35→ 36→1. **Identify the quote type:** Check if apostrophes/quotes in strings are curly (`'` `"`) or straight (`'` `"`) 37→2. **If straight quotes inside strings:** Must escape them with backslash 38→3. **Common patterns to escape:** 39→ - Contractions: `I'm` → `I\'m`, `it's` → `it\'s`, `they're` → `they\'re` 40→ - Possessives: `Greg's` → `Greg\'s` 41→ - Measurements: `5'8"` → `5\'8"` 42→ - Past tense: `I've` → `I\'ve`, `hasn't` → `hasn\'t` 43→ 44→### Prevention 45→ 46→- Use a text editor that preserves Unicode characters 47→- Don't "normalize" quotes unless you also escape them 48→- Test PHP syntax after any string modifications 49→- Consider using double-quoted strings with variable interpolation instead 50→ 51→### Related Documentation 52→ 53→- [MODERNIZATION_PATTERN.md - String Escaping](MODERNIZATION_PATTERN.md#pattern-string-escaping) 54→ 55→--- 56→ 57→## Issue #2: Foreach Loop Syntax Preference 58→ 59→**Discovered:** Multiple template modernizations 60→ 61→### Problem 62→ 63→The codebase uses alternative syntax (`:` / `endforeach;`) but user prefers curly brace syntax. 64→ 65→### Example 66→ 67→```php 68→// Original 69→ 70→
71→ 72→ 73→// Preferred 74→ 75→
76→ 77→``` 78→ 79→### Solution 80→ 81→Always use curly braces `{ }` for control structures when modernizing templates. 82→ 83→--- 84→ 85→## Issue #3: Missing Semicolons in Arrays 86→ 87→**Discovered:** page-overview-multiquiz.php (line 334) 88→ 89→### Problem 90→ 91→Array declarations missing semicolons at end of variable assignments. 92→ 93→### Example 94→ 95→```php 96→// Missing semicolon - WRONG 97→$age = $review['age'] 98→ 99→// With semicolon - CORRECT 100→$age = $review['age']; 101→``` 102→ 103→### Solution 104→ 105→Always add semicolons after variable assignments, even inside foreach loops. 106→ 107→--- 108→ 109→## Issue #4: Template Name Naming Consistency 110→ 111→**Discovered:** mens-hrt-cc-why-seek.php (commit 3b783fc) 112→ 113→### Problem 114→ 115→Template names must match exactly across all files or assets won't load. 116→ 117→### Requirements 118→ 119→All of these MUST match exactly: 120→- PHP filename: `page-[template-name].php` 121→- `$template_name` variable value 122→- SCSS filename: `[template-name].scss` 123→- functions.php array entry: `'[template-name]'` 124→- JS bundle: `[template-name].bundle.js` 125→ 126→### Example 127→ 128→``` 129→✅ CORRECT - All match: 130→- page-mens-hrt-cc-why-seek.php 131→- $template_name = 'mens-hrt-cc-why-seek'; 132→- mens-hrt-cc-why-seek.scss 133→- 'mens-hrt-cc-why-seek' in functions.php 134→ 135→❌ WRONG - Mismatches: 136→- page-mens-hrt-cc-why-seek.php 137→- $template_name = 'mens_hrt_cc_why_seek'; // Underscores! 138→- mens-hrt-cc-whyseek.scss // Missing hyphen! 139→``` 140→ 141→### Solution 142→ 143→Use exact naming convention: lowercase with hyphens, no underscores or variations. 144→ 145→--- 146→ 147→## Issue #5: Pricing Variable Not Updated Throughout 148→ 149→**Discovered:** page-overview-multiquiz.php 150→ 151→### Problem 152→ 153→Templates often display pricing in multiple places. Must update ALL occurrences. 154→ 155→### Common Locations 156→ 157→1. Sticky CTA section (top) 158→2. "What's Included" pricing breakdown 159→3. Total cost display 160→4. Any promotional banners 161→ 162→### Solution 163→ 164→1. Define pricing variables at top of template 165→2. Search for hardcoded prices (`$150`, `$45`, etc.) 166→3. Replace all occurrences with variables 167→4. Verify with visual inspection 168→ 169→```php 170→// Define once 171→$original_price = '$150'; 172→$discounted_price = '$45'; 173→ 174→// Use everywhere 175→ 176→ 177→``` 178→ 179→--- 180→ 181→## Issue #6: Backup Template Naming 182→ 183→**Discovered:** All template modernizations 184→ 185→### Problem 186→ 187→Backup `.old.php` files need to be distinguishable from modernized templates in the WordPress template dropdown to avoid confusion. 188→ 189→### Symptoms 190→ 191→- Multiple templates with identical names in WordPress 192→- Confusion about which template is active 193→- Accidentally using old template instead of modernized one 194→ 195→### Solution 196→ 197→Always append "- OLD" to the template name in backup files: 198→ 199→```php 200→// Original/Modernized template 201→ 202→ 203→// Backup file (.old.php) 204→ 205→``` 206→ 207→This makes it clear in the WordPress admin which template is the legacy version. 208→ 209→### Implementation 210→ 211→After creating the backup: 212→ 213→```bash 214→# 1. Create backup 215→cp src/page-overview-multiquiz.php src/page-overview-multiquiz.old.php 216→ 217→# 2. Edit the .old.php file and update line 1: 218→# From: 219→# To: 220→``` 221→ 222→### Benefits 223→ 224→- Clear differentiation in WordPress template dropdown 225→- Easy to identify legacy vs modernized templates 226→- Prevents accidental use of old template 227→- Maintains backup for reference without conflict 228→ 229→--- 230→ 231→## Issue #7: Trustpilot Script Placement 232→ 233→**Discovered:** page-overview-multiquiz.php, page-mens-hrt-cc-buybox.php, page-mens-hrt-cc-why-seek.php 234→ 235→### Problem 236→ 237→Templates that use Trustpilot widgets have a script tag in the `` that needs to be preserved but moved to the correct location in the modernized template. 238→ 239→### Symptoms 240→ 241→- Trustpilot widgets don't render 242→- Console error: "Trustpilot is not defined" 243→- Empty space where widgets should appear 244→ 245→### Original Location 246→ 247→In legacy templates, the Trustpilot script is in the ``: 248→ 249→```php 250→ 251→ 252→ 253→ 254→``` 255→ 256→### Solution 257→ 258→**Move the script to just before `get_footer()`:** 259→ 260→```php 261→ 262→ 263→ 264→ 265→ 266→``` 267→ 268→### Why This Location? 269→ 270→1. **After content:** The script needs to load after the page content where Trustpilot widgets are placed 271→2. **Before footer:** Ensures widgets initialize before the page finishes loading 272→3. **Not in header.php:** This is template-specific, not all templates use Trustpilot 273→ 274→### How to Check 275→ 276→1. Search the original template for "trustpilot" (case-insensitive) 277→2. If found in ``, move it to before `get_footer()` 278→3. If not found, no action needed 279→ 280→```bash 281→# Quick check: 282→grep -i "trustpilot" src/page-[template].old.php 283→``` 284→ 285→### Related Widgets 286→ 287→This same pattern applies to other third-party widget scripts that need to be loaded before the footer but aren't universal across all templates. 288→ 289→--- 290→ 291→## Issue #8: Slick Carousel Not Initializing (jQuery Pattern) 292→ 293→**Discovered:** page-overview-multiquiz.php 294→ 295→### Problem 296→ 297→Slick carousel doesn't initialize because jQuery isn't available when the script runs. This happens when using `$(document).ready()` directly instead of the proper jQuery availability check pattern. 298→ 299→### Symptoms 300→ 301→- Carousel shows all slides vertically stacked 302→- Console error: `$ is not a function` or `$(...).slick is not a function` 303→- Slick dots/arrows don't appear 304→ 305→### Wrong Pattern 306→ 307→```javascript 308→// WRONG - Assumes $ is available globally 309→$(document).ready(function() { 310→ $('.carousel').slick({...}); 311→}); 312→``` 313→ 314→### Correct Pattern 315→ 316→```javascript 317→// CORRECT - Checks jQuery availability and uses safe reference 318→if (typeof jQuery !== 'undefined') { 319→ jQuery(document).ready(function($) { 320→ $('.carousel').slick({...}); 321→ }); 322→} 323→``` 324→ 325→### Why This Matters 326→ 327→1. `$` may not be defined globally if jQuery is loaded with `noConflict` 328→2. The script may execute before jQuery is fully loaded 329→3. Using `jQuery(document).ready(function($)` provides a safe local reference to `$` 330→ 331→### Full Example (Reviews Carousel) 332→ 333→```javascript 334→/** 335→ * ============================= 336→ * CUSTOM PAGE-SPECIFIC JQUERY 337→ * ============================= 338→ */ 339→if (typeof jQuery !== 'undefined') { 340→ jQuery(document).ready(function($) { 341→ /** 342→ * ---------------------------- 343→ * REVIEWS CAROUSEL 344→ * ---------------------------- 345→ */ 346→ $('.carousel').slick({ 347→ slidesToShow: 3, 348→ infinite: true, 349→ slidesToScroll: 1, 350→ dots: true, 351→ arrows: false, 352→ responsive: [{ 353→ breakpoint: 768, 354→ settings: { 355→ slidesToShow: 1, 356→ slidesToScroll: 1 357→ } 358→ }, 359→ { 360→ breakpoint: 1280, 361→ settings: { 362→ slidesToShow: 2, 363→ slidesToScroll: 1 364→ } 365→ }] 366→ }); 367→ }); 368→} 369→``` 370→ 371→### Templates Using This Pattern 372→ 373→- ✅ page-mens-hrt-cc-buybox.php 374→- ✅ page-mens-hrt-cc-why-seek.php 375→- ✅ page-overview-multiquiz.php (fixed) 376→ 377→--- 378→ 379→## Issue #9: Font-Face Declarations in Legacy SCSS 380→ 381→**Discovered:** January 7, 2026 during batch template modernization 382→ 383→### Problem 384→ 385→Legacy CSS files contain `@font-face` declarations that duplicate font definitions already present in `main.css`. Including these in legacy SCSS mixins causes unnecessary duplication and increases CSS file size. 386→ 387→### Symptoms 388→ 389→- Larger than expected compiled CSS files 390→- Duplicate font-face declarations in compiled CSS 391→- Font files loaded multiple times 392→ 393→### Example 394→ 395→```scss 396→// WRONG - Don't include in legacy mixin 397→@mixin legacy-styles { 398→ @font-face { 399→ font-family: "DM Sans"; 400→ src: url("../../fonts/DMSans-VariableFont_opsz,wght.woff2") format("woff2"); 401→ font-weight: 400 500; 402→ font-style: normal; 403→ font-display: swap; 404→ } 405→ 406→ html, body, div { ... } 407→} 408→ 409→// CORRECT - Start directly with CSS rules 410→@mixin legacy-styles { 411→ html, body, div { ... } 412→} 413→``` 414→ 415→### Solution 416→ 417→When extracting legacy CSS to SCSS mixins: 418→ 419→1. **Remove all @font-face declarations** - they're already in `main.css` 420→2. Start the mixin content with the first non-font-face CSS rule (usually the CSS reset starting with `html, body, div...`) 421→3. Keep all other CSS rules intact 422→ 423→### How to Fix Existing Files 424→ 425→```bash 426→# Remove lines 7-124 (all @font-face declarations) from the file 427→# Keep the @mixin wrapper and everything after the font declarations 428→``` 429→ 430→### Prevention 431→ 432→- Always check for `@font-face` at the start of legacy CSS files 433→- Remove them before wrapping in the mixin 434→- Font faces are centralized in `main.css` and don't need to be repeated 435→ 436→### Related Files 437→ 438→Files that had font-faces removed: 439→- ✅ `page-overview-multiquiz.scss` (removed 118 lines of font declarations) 440→- ✅ `overview-pricing.scss` (already clean) 441→- ✅ `overview-3.scss` (already clean) 442→- ✅ `mens-opt-comparison-forbes.scss` (needs checking) 443→ 444→--- 445→ 446→## Best Practices Summary 447→ 448→1. **Create backups with " - OLD" suffix** in template name to differentiate in WordPress 449→2. **Check for Trustpilot script** in `` and move to before `get_footer()` if present 450→3. **Use proper jQuery pattern** for Slick carousels: `if (typeof jQuery !== 'undefined') { jQuery(document).ready(function($) {...}); }` 451→4. **Always escape straight quotes** inside strings that use matching quote delimiters 452→5. **Use curly braces** for control structures (`foreach`, `if`, `while`) 453→6. **Check semicolons** after all statements 454→7. **Match naming exactly** across all files and references 455→8. **Update pricing consistently** throughout the template 456→9. **Remove @font-face declarations** from legacy SCSS files (fonts are in main.css) 457→10. **Update relative paths** in legacy SCSS from `../` to `../../` 458→11. **Test PHP syntax** after making changes 459→12. **Visual regression test** to ensure nothing broke 460→13. **Check console** for JavaScript errors 461→ 462→--- 463→ 464→## Testing Checklist After Fixing Issues 465→ 466→- [ ] PHP syntax check: `php -l [template-file].php` 467→- [ ] Build assets: `yarn build` 468→- [ ] Visual inspection: Page looks correct 469→- [ ] Console check: No JavaScript errors 470→- [ ] Interactive elements: Quiz, carousel, accordion all work 471→- [ ] Links work: All CTAs point to correct URLs 472→- [ ] Mobile responsive: Check all breakpoints 473→ 474→--- 475→ 476→## Need Help? 477→ 478→1. Review [MODERNIZATION_PATTERN.md](MODERNIZATION_PATTERN.md) 479→2. Check [BEFORE_AFTER_EXAMPLES.md](BEFORE_AFTER_EXAMPLES.md) 480→3. Look at similar completed templates in `changes/` 481→4. When in doubt, preserve original code structure 482→ 483→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a final summary document:

## Comprehensive Exploration Complete

I've thoroughly explored the entire `.local/template-modernization/` directory and the `src/` directory structure. Here's the complete documentation package:

### DOCUMENTATION IN `.local/template-modernization/`

#### **Core Documentation Files:**

1. **README.md** - Overview and purpose of the modernization effort
   - Branch: jb/17083-modernize-legacy-templates-pt1

2. **INDEX.md** - Complete navigation and learning path
   - Documents all 11 files

   - Learning path for AI assistants (3 phases: understand, see examples, practice)

3. **MODERNIZATION_PATTERN.md** - Step-by-step process (450 lines)
   - 10-step detailed process
   - 1. Create backup with " - OLD" suffix
   - 2. Extract legacy styles to SCSS mixin
   - 3. Create page-specific SCSS structure
   - 4. Transform PHP template header (variables + get_header())
   - 5. Clean up template body
   - 6. Transform PHP template footer (get_footer() only)
   - 7. Update functions.php
   - 8. Handle JavaScript
   - 9. Build and test
   - 10. Commit changes
   - Includes 12 common patterns and troubleshooting guide
   - **CRITICAL:** Explains PHP string escaping for straight quotes (`\'`)
   - Covers Trustpilot script placement, query string persistence

4. **QUICK_REFERENCE.md** - Fast lookup guide (425 lines)
   - Copy/paste code templates for:
     - Template header
     - Template footer
     - Page-specific SCSS
     - Legacy SCSS wrapper
     - functions.php update
   - File naming convention requirements
   - Complete checklist (25 items)
   - Common gotchas and decision trees
   - Build commands and testing URLs
   - File locations reference
   - Existing legacy stylesheets table
   - Time estimates by scenario

5. **BEFORE_AFTER_EXAMPLES.md** - Real code transformations (500 lines)
   - 8 detailed before/after code comparisons
   - Backup file template naming
   - Template header transformation (full example from article-trt-pmax)
   - Template footer transformation (with and without Trustpilot)
   - CSS architecture transformation (legacy mixin + page-specific)
   - JavaScript enqueuing transformation
   - functions.php update example
   - Shared legacy styles example (multiple templates using same SCSS)
   - Linkbar integration example
   - Complete file structure comparison

6. **COMMON_ISSUES.md** - 9 documented issues + solutions (480 lines)
   - Issue #1: PHP string escaping (curly vs straight quotes)
   - Issue #2: Foreach loop syntax preference
   - Issue #3: Missing semicolons in arrays
   - Issue #4: Template name naming consistency (must match everywhere)
   - Issue #5: Pricing variable not updated throughout
   - Issue #6: Backup template naming
   - Issue #7: Trustpilot script placement
   - Issue #8: Slick carousel jQuery pattern
   - Issue #9: Font-Face declarations in legacy SCSS
   - Best practices summary (10 items)
   - Testing checklist

#### **Template Changelog Files:**

1. **TEMPLATE.md** - Template for documenting new modernizations
   - Structure showing all sections to document
   - Metrics template
   - Files created/modified/reused sections
   - Template changes breakdown
   - CSS and JavaScript architecture sections
   - Testing notes format
   - Performance impact section
   - Lessons learned section

2. **page-article-trt-pmax.md** - First simple template (259 lines)
   - 718 → 567 lines (21% reduction)
   - Single legacy stylesheet (article-hrt-overview.scss, 2,385 lines)
   - Commits: 3f7e514, bd428b7
   - Extracted legacy styles, created page SCSS, modernized PHP header/footer
   - Added linkbar integration
   - Global variables: $class_names, $linkbar_vars
   - Successfully demonstrates basic pattern

3. **page-mens-hrt-cc-buybox.md** - Complex template with quiz (377 lines)
   - 1,158 → 791 lines (32% reduction)
   - **Two legacy stylesheets:**
     - page-overview-multiquiz.scss (4,717 lines)
     - care-plans-carousel.scss (419 lines)
   - 5 commits: c0a2e5e, dec2d4c, 424010b, 509a75f, yb
   - Complex features: embedded quiz, care plans carousel, statistics, Trustpilot
   - Uses `add_action` method for JS enqueuing (alternative pattern)
   - Demonstrates multiple legacy stylesheet imports in single page SCSS

4. **page-mens-hrt-cc-why-seek.md** - Similar template reuse (319 lines)
   - 1,158 → 791 lines (32% reduction)
   - **Reuses same two legacy stylesheets** as buybox
   - Demonstrates key benefit: CSS duplication reduction (~90% if scaled to 10 templates)
   - Shows pattern efficiency improvement (60% faster than first similar template)
   - File naming issue fixed in commit 3b783fc (hyphens consistency)
   - Validates pattern scalability

5. **page-overview-multiquiz.md** - 10-question quiz template (380 lines)
   - 910 → 491 lines (46% reduction)
   - **Reused existing legacy stylesheet** (page-overview-multiquiz.scss)
   - Complex features: 10-question quiz, reviews carousel, accordion FAQ
   - **Critical lesson:** String escaping for straight quotes (I\'m, It\'s, they\'re)
   - Post-modernization fixes documented:
     - jQuery pattern for Slick carousel
     - Trustpilot script placement
   - Demonstrates fastest modernization (45 min vs 2+ hours for first similar)

6. **page-overview-light-rebrand.md** - Light rebrand template (290 lines)
   - 1,075 → 602 lines (44% reduction)
   - Date: January 5, 2026
   - **Two legacy stylesheets:**
     - page-overview-multiquiz.scss (4,717 lines base)
     - page-overview-light-rebrand.scss (2,510 lines rebrand overrides)
   - Complex features: 5-question quiz, product carousel, reviews carousel, accordion, animated stats
   - Uses `wp_localize_script` to pass theme folder to JS
   - Standardized header comments in SCSS and JS files
   - Demonstrates layered legacy styles (base + rebrand overrides)

### ACTUAL CODEBASE STRUCTURE IN `src/`

#### **Key PHP Files:**

1. **functions.php** (166 lines)
   - `HONE_PAGE_TEMPLATES` array with 16 templates (as of current version)
   - `hone_landing_enqueue_assets()` function handles CSS/JS loading
   - CSS loading logic: checks if template matches array, enqueues page-specific CSS
   - `hone_register_bundles()` function registers all page bundles with dependencies
   - `get_query_string()` helper for persisting query parameters
   - jQuery Migrate and Slick carousel registered as dependencies

2. **header.php** (42 lines shown)
   - Declares global $class_names, $checkout_url, $linkbar_vars
   - Injects page title into $class_names array
   - Sets up centralized <head> with:
     - Optimizely script
     - Google Tag Manager
     - Meta tags
     - Font preloads
   - Opens <main> tag with dynamic classes: `implode(' ', array_unique($class_names))`
   - Includes linkbar template part if $linkbar_vars is set
   - Includes navbar template part

3. **footer.php** (35 lines)
   - Closes </main> tag
   - Renders footer HTML:
     - Hone logo
     - LegitScript approval seal
     - Disclaimers
     - Copyright and footer links
   - Calls `wp_footer()` to load enqueued scripts
   - Closes </body> and </html>

#### **Page Templates:**

- ~40 page templates total in both src/ and build/
- Mix of legacy (unremodernized) and modernized templates
- Modernized templates are much shorter (567-791 lines vs 1000+ for legacy)
- Legacy templates still have inline HTML/CSS/JS

#### **Template Parts** (`template-parts/`):
- `linkbar.php` - Optional sticky linkbar component
- `navbar.php` - Navigation bar
- `carousel-navigation.php` - Carousel controls
- `cta-block.php` - CTA button blocks
- `image.php` - Image helper
- `percentage-circle.php` - Animated percentage circles
- `slick-navigation.php` - Slick carousel navigation
- `x-in-x-stat.php` - Statistics display (e.g., "1 in 4")

#### **SCSS Structure:**

**main.scss** (11 lines) - Entry point that imports:
- `main/reset` - CSS reset
- `main/utilities/borders` - Border utilities
- `settings/_index` - All settings (fonts, buttons, layout, spacing, borders, common)
- `parts/_index` - Shared component styles (navbar, linkbar, footer, carousel, etc.)

**Directory structure:**
- `main/` - Global styles (reset, utilities)
- `settings/` - Theme settings (fonts.scss, buttons.scss, layout.scss, etc.)
- `parts/` - Shared component styles (navbar, linkbar, footer)
- `pages/` - 16 page-specific SCSS files (each imports legacy styles if needed)
- `legacy/styles/` - 6 legacy SCSS mixins (reusable across multiple templates):
  - `overview-pricing.scss` (81KB, 2000+ lines)
  - `overview-3.scss` (80KB)
  - `womens-hormone-imbalance.scss` (80KB)
  - `mens-opt-comparison-forbes.scss` (82KB)
  - `infographic-carousel.scss` (12KB)
  - `monitor-indicators-paralax.scss` (2.8KB)
- `variables/` - Color, font, and layout variables

**Page-specific SCSS pattern:**
```scss
@use '../legacy/styles/[legacy-file]' as legacy-styles;

// BASIC OVERRIDES
// (applied before legacy)

@include legacy-styles.legacy-styles();

// CUSTOM STYLES
// (applied after legacy, highest specificity)
```

#### **JavaScript Structure:**

**Utilities** (`static/js/utilities/`):
- `accordion.js` - Accordion toggle functionality
- `carousel.js` - Slick carousel wrapper
- `quiz.js` - Quiz framework
- `stickyCTA.js` - Sticky CTA scroll handler

**Page Bundles** (`static/js/pages/`):
- Individual `.bundle.js` files for each page template
- Bundled with esbuild
- Include page-specific logic (quiz, carousel init, accordion, etc.)
- jQuery is external (loaded by WordPress)

#### **Build Configuration:**

1. **esbuild.build.mjs** (53 lines)
   - Compiles main combined.js (backward compatibility)
   - Bundles all page-specific JS bundles
   - Minifies output
   - External: jQuery (not bundled)
   - Output format: IIFE (Immediately Invoked Function Expression)

2. **build.js** (43 lines)
   - Cleans /build/ directory
   - Runs `yarn build:assets` (SCSS + JS compilation)
   - Copies src/ to build/, excluding:
     - .scss files
     - scss directory
     - js/modules directory
     - js/index.js

### KEY PATTERNS & CONVENTIONS

1. **Naming Convention (Critical - Must Match Exactly):**
   - PHP: `page-{slug}.php` where slug uses hyphens (e.g., `article-trt-pmax`)
   - Template variable: `$template_name = '{slug}'`
   - SCSS: `src/static/scss/pages/{slug}.scss`
   - JS bundle: `src/static/js/pages/{slug}.bundle.js`
   - functions.php array: `'{slug}'`
   - CSS output: `{slug}.css`
   - JS output: `{slug}.bundle.min.js`

2. **Global Variables:**
   - `$class_names[]` - Array of CSS classes for <main> tag
   - `$linkbar_vars[]` - Optional configuration for sticky linkbar
   - `$theme_folder` - Theme URI for asset paths
   - `$qs` - Query string persistence

3. **CSS Architecture:**
   - main.min.css - Global styles (loaded on all pages)
   - pages/{slug}.css - Page-specific styles (auto-enqueued if template in HONE_PAGE_TEMPLATES)
   - Legacy styles organized in reusable SCSS mixins

4. **JavaScript Patterns:**
   - Direct enqueue: `wp_enqueue_script('hone-page-' . $template_name);`
   - Add action: `add_action('wp_enqueue_scripts', function() use ($template_name) { wp_enqueue_script(...); });`
   - jQuery availability check: `if (typeof jQuery !== 'undefined') { jQuery(document).ready(function($) { ... }); }`
   - Pass data to JS: `wp_localize_script('handle', 'honePageData', array('themeFolder' => $theme_folder))`

5. **Trustpilot Script:**
   - Originally in <head> tag
   - Must be moved to just before `get_footer()`
   - Needs to load before footer renders

6. **Backup Template Naming:**
   - Always append " - OLD" to backup file template name
   - Prevents confusion in WordPress template selector
   - Example: Original "Overview Multiquiz" → Backup "Overview Multiquiz - OLD"

### METRICS & STATISTICS

**Modernization Progress:**
- Templates completed: 5 out of ~40 (12.5%)
- Code reduction per template: 21-46% (average 32%)
- Time per template: 30-120 minutes depending on complexity

**Legacy SCSS Files (Reusable):**
- article-hrt-overview.scss - 2,385 lines (used by article templates)
- page-overview-multiquiz.scss - 4,717 lines (used by 3+ templates)
- page-overview-light-rebrand.scss - 2,510 lines (rebrand overrides)
- care-plans-carousel.scss - 419 lines (carousel styles)
- overview-pricing.scss - 2000+ lines (pricing templates)
- (Plus 2 more legacy files)

**CSS Duplication Reduction:**
- Before modernization: Each template had duplicate CSS
- After modernization: CSS shared via SCSS mixins
- Potential 90% reduction if 10 templates share same styles
